title: Sub-ecosystem and archive richness phase
summary: Thirty-second structured packet for Eco Explorer. After the current comparison and close-look wave, lane 2 should deepen the newly opened sub-ecosystem spaces and the game's archive surfaces through authored content, notes, close-look additions, and science-safe payoff passes while staying mostly inside content-owned files.

execution_order.goal: Prepare an old-growth and cave support content pack.
execution_order.why_now: Lane 3's newer spaces need content density as soon as they prove out.

execution_order.goal: Implement the old-growth and cave support content pack.
execution_order.why_now: New sub-ecosystem spaces should feel alive, not just spatially larger.

execution_order.goal: Review the sub-ecosystem content support pack.
execution_order.why_now: Science accuracy and readability remain the lane's main gate.

execution_order.goal: Prepare an atlas and sketchbook archive-richness pass.
execution_order.why_now: Logged routes and expeditions should feel more personal through existing archive surfaces.

execution_order.goal: Add atlas and sketchbook archive richness.
execution_order.why_now: The game's growing chapter structure needs stronger notebook payoff.
Archive payoff should stay compact and content-first.

execution_order.goal: Prepare process-backed notes and close-look candidates.
execution_order.why_now: Existing process, note, and close-look systems can carry one more content wave without new UI.

execution_order.goal: Add process-backed note and close-look richness.
execution_order.why_now: This helps the world feel more observant and seasonal using systems that are already live.
The lane should only keep going if the additions stay calm and science-safe.

execution_order.goal: Run one final quiet science-ledger and payoff sweep.
execution_order.why_now: A small closing sweep helps the lane leave behind clean content support rather than just more entries.

execution_order.goal: Review the full follow-on lane-2 richness wave.
execution_order.why_now: This is the quality gate before lane 2 spins into another broader content phase.

guardrails: Stay mostly inside src/content, science docs, and content-facing tests.
Avoid route-board shell changes, field-station structure changes, and broader progression logic.
Use existing note, comparison, close-look, sketchbook, and atlas systems.
Keep science accuracy and kid readability as hard gates.
Prefer meaningful content support for new spaces over broad undirected biome sprawl.
For the first sub-ecosystem support pass, favor one cave landmark, one old-growth-specific landmark, and tighter notebook synthesis over a broad new species wave.
For the first archive-richness pass, keep the atlas shell unchanged and use filed-memory copy plus route-defining sketchbook notes instead of a new archive panel.
For the process-backed follow-on, favor treeline and tundra process-teaching notes plus two visual-first close-look additions instead of reopening forest microhabitats or widening comparisons.
Do not spend the process-backed follow-on on new observation prompt seeds; the current prompt seed seam cannot filter on phenology without systems work.
acceptance_focus: New sub-ecosystem spaces feel richer as soon as they land.
Atlas and sketchbook payoff gets stronger without a new UI shell.
handoff_prompt: Read AGENTS.md, .agents/lane-runner.md, .agents/project-memory.md, .agents/work-queue.md, .agents/lanes/lane-2.md, docs/reports/2026-03-30-sub-ecosystem-and-archive-richness-phase.md, docs/reports/2026-03-30-old-growth-and-cave-support-pack-handoff.md, docs/reports/2026-03-30-atlas-and-sketchbook-archive-richness-handoff.md, docs/reports/2026-03-30-process-backed-note-and-close-look-handoff.md, and this packet. Treat lane 2's next wave as a content-owned follow-on: keep the new forest microhabitat work parked, use one alpine-only process-teaching pass to add a treeline freeze-thaw note, a tundra thaw-edge note, and two visual-first close-look entries, and avoid new prompt-seed or comparison expansion in this step.
